claim.content:
Consider a three-dimensional interacting SU($N_{c}$) Fermi gas at low temperature where Landau Fermi-liquid phenomenology applies. Let $M$ be the bare particle mass, $M^{*}$ the quasiparticle effective mass on the Fermi surface, and $k_{\mu}\equiv\sqrt{2M\mu}$. The proposition is: to second order in the small parameter $k_{\mu}a$, the quasiparticle effective mass is given by
      $
      \displaystyle \frac{M^{*}}{M}=1+\left(N_{c}-1\right)\frac{8}{15\pi^{2}}(7\ln 2-1)\left(k_{\mu}a\right)^{2},
      $
      provided that the system is in the Fermi-liquid regime (no phase transitions or strong-coupling breakdown) and that vertex corrections beyond the order shown are negligible in the weak-coupling limit.
      [32]
